name: Grinnell System
description: An extreme, fast-paced basketball strategy developed at Grinnell College. It relies on a mathematical formula that prioritizes three-point shots, offensive rebounds, and forcing turnovers over defensive stops. The system's core tenets include: taking the first available shot (preferably a 3-pointer within 12 seconds), crashing the offensive glass with all non-shooters, applying relentless full-court press, and substituting players in 5-man "hockey shifts" every 1-2 minutes to maintain absolute maximum energy.
